articles.title: Ex-Meta employee shares viral post claiming non-Chinese workers felt targeted during mass layoffs
articles.description: A former Meta engineer has detailed feelings of social and professional isolation within certain teams, claiming non-Chinese employees were excluded due to colleagues predominantly speaking Mandarin. He contrasted this with a positive experience in South Korea, where English was used to include him. The engineer emphasized he is not targeting the Chinese community.

articles.title: Karnataka, Bengaluru weather update: Bangalore to receive rain, thunderstorms till May 25; IMD warns of intense showers across state
articles.description: Weather in Bengaluru: Bengaluru is under a yellow alert for intense rainfall until May 25, with thunderstorms and lightning expected. Pre-monsoon showers will continue across Karnataka, bringing cloudy skies and light to moderate rain to multiple districts. Some areas may experience heavy downpours and gusty winds, impacting daily life and travel.

articles.title: Can ‘broken heart syndrome’ be cured? Woman who developed condition after mother’s death joins new drug trial
articles.description: heartbreak has officially entered the realm of medical concerns. Researchers in the UK are launching an innovative trial targeting 'broken heart syndrome' (takotsubo cardiomyopathy), a condition that resembles a heart attack but is rooted in extreme emotional or physical strain. This condition is notably more prevalent among women over 50.

articles.title: Nancy Guthrie Case: ‘Mastermind hackers’ or ‘street level thugs’? Former detective comes up with ‘wrench attack’ theory as investigators scramble for breakthrough
articles.description: The 16-week investigation into Nancy Guthrie's disappearance continues without suspects. Authorities suspect the 84-year-old was abducted from her Arizona home. Experts suggest a shift in cybercriminal tactics, with some now employing physical violence. While the case has sophisticated elements, a former detective believes "street-level thugs" might be involved, contrasting with traditional "wrench attacks."

articles.description: Hero MotoCorp expects to maintain its market leadership and deliver double-digit growth in FY27, driven by strong demand for scooters, electric vehicles, exports and premium motorcycles, CEO Harshavardhan Chitale said.

articles.title: “I never wanted kids”: Dwyane Wade’s wife Gabrielle Union breaks silence on how the NBA legend changed her perspective on motherhood
articles.description: Dwyane Wade, the NBA legend, and his wife, renowned actress Gabrielle Union, have remained united even as they fought for years against her fertility issues as they tried to have a family together. While both Dwyane Wade and Gabrielle Union appear fulfilled in life with their only daughter, Kaavia Wade, the actress has often spoken about how she never wanted to be a mother initially.

articles.title: Raul Castro | The old man and the siege
articles.description: The Trump administration, which has imposed a punishing blockade on Cuba, has indicted Raul Castro Ruz, the 94-year old former revolutionary leader and President, in a replay of the farcical accusations that led to the U.S. abduction of Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro early this year.

articles.title: Rajya Sabha poll math revives Gowda renomination talk
articles.description: Four Rajya Sabha seats from Karnataka will be decided in polls on June 18. Congress is expected to secure three seats. The BJP and JD(S) alliance is likely to get one seat. This has led to discussions about the BJP supporting former Prime Minister HD Deve Gowda for another term.

articles.title: Rozgar Mela: Joshi gives 150 letters in Hubballi, 51k nationwide
articles.description: Union Minister Pralhad Joshi distributed 150 appointment letters in Hubballi as part of the Rozgar Mela. PM Modi led the nationwide drive, distributing 51,000 letters and hailing the growth of self-employment and India's startup ecosystem.

articles.title: CSIR-IIIM hosts Prof Serge Mignani to boost drug discovery research
articles.description: CSIR-IIIM Jammu hosted scientist Prof Serge Mignani for a collaborative meeting to advance partnerships in drug discovery. Discussions focused on anti-TB, oncology, and inflammation research, aiming to boost Indo-French scientific cooperation.

articles.title: US-India relationship foundational for Washington's strategy: Rubio
articles.description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio called the US-India relationship a 'foundational element' of Washington's strategy. During his visit, he reaffirmed ties, hinted at new announcements, and is set to meet PM Modi and EAM Jaishankar to discuss key issues.

articles.title: Assam's growth: Sarma meets FM, inaugurates Syama Prasad flyover
articles.description: Assam CM Himanta Biswa Sarma met Union FM Nirmala Sitharaman to discuss the state's development. He also inaugurated the ₹376 crore Dr. Syama Prasad Mookerjee Flyover in Guwahati and took oath for his 6th term as MLA.

articles.title: Nagarjuna marks 40 years in cinema, cherishes May 23 memories
articles.description: Superstar Nagarjuna Akkineni cherishes May 23, the release date of his debut film 'Vikram' and his father ANR's last film 'Manam'. He marked 40 years in cinema with a heartfelt note, with son Naga Chaitanya also paying tribute.

articles.title: Char Dham Yatra: Uttarakhand CS reviews arrangements for pilgrims
articles.description: Uttarakhand Chief Secretary Anand Bardhan reviewed Char Dham Yatra arrangements, directing DMs to streamline the pilgrimage. He suggested night darshan for crowds and ordered monsoon preparedness, including evacuation plans and health screenings.

articles.title: MI coach Jayawardene defends Hardik Pandya's on-field frustrations
articles.description: MI head coach Mahela Jayawardene addressed the team's frustrating IPL season, noting captain Hardik Pandya's struggles with injury and on-field anger. He said the team failed to execute despite having the talent and squad to perform well.

articles.title: MDMK's Vaiko condemns Coimbatore girl's murder, demands strict action
articles.description: MDMK chief Vaiko condemned the abduction and murder of a 10-year-old girl in Coimbatore. Demanding strict action, he called for a law mandating life imprisonment for such crimes and urged the state government to ensure the safety of women and children.
